items.title: No individual allowed to undermine national efforts to combat IUU fishing: PM
items.snippet: Prime Minister Pham Minh Chinh has stressed that wrongdoings by any individual or organisation must not be allowed to affect the country’s overall efforts to have the European Commission’s “yellow card” warning lifted.

items.title: Acting minister urges EAEU to further open market for Vietnamese aquatic products
items.snippet: Both host and guest agreed to facilitate market access, promptly remove trade barriers and address difficulties in order to meet cooperation needs and fully tap the economic potential of the EAEU and Vietnam.

items.title: PM chairs 32nd meeting of national steering committee for anti-IUU fishing
items.snippet: The highest objective is to meet the dual requirement of lifting the EC’s yellow-card warning and ensuring the sustainable development of the fisheries sector, PM Chinh underscored.

items.title: Khanh Hoa ensures transparency in data to address IUU fishing
items.snippet: Determined to have the EC’s “yellow card” warning lifted, Khanh Hoa has implemented drastic measures against IUU fishing in recent years.

items.title: Captain charged in illegal fishing case linked to Philippine waters
items.snippet: While operating at sea, fishing vessel PY-96896-TS, captained by Toai, allegedly removed its vessel monitoring system (VMS) device and transferred it to fishing vessel PY-90479-TS, captained by his younger brother, in an attempt to evade supervision and tracking by the Vietnam fisheries surveillance force. Toai then st

items.title: ‘Borneo states key to climate plan’
items.snippet: KUCHING: The forests and rich biodiver­sity of Sabah and Sarawak play a central role in Malaysia's climate strategy, says Sarawak Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Openg.

items.title: Pagasa: El Niño may develop August to October, intense habagat looms
items.snippet: [Updated March 3, 2026, 7:32 p.m.] MANILA, Philippines — The El Niño phenomenon may start developing by August to October this year, according to the Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (Pagasa). In an interview with the INQUIRER, Pagasa Climate Monitoring and Prediction Section

items.title: Myanmar elections marked by fear as UN warns civilians are coerced from all sides
items.snippet: As Myanmar approaches elections scheduled for 28 December, the UN’s top human rights official has said that civilians are being coerced from all sides – forced by the military to vote and threatened by armed opposition groups to boycott – in a climate of fear, violence and mass repression.

items.title: PMI hits four-month high in February
items.snippet: Manufacturing production increased rapidly in February, with the rate of expansion quickening to a 19-month high. Panellists reported that the preparation of products ahead of delivery to clients and stronger customer demand were behind the latest rise in output.

items.title: Takiyuddin apologises for linking maritime op to Memali incident
items.snippet: Dewan Rakyat speaker accepts the PN chief whip's apology after he admits his error.

items.title: Vietnam Border Guard - the nation’s steel shield
items.snippet: On March 3, 1959, the People’s Armed Police Force - now the Vietnam Border Guard - was officially established. The event marked an important milestone in the strategic policy of the Party and the State regarding the protection of national sovereignty and border security.

items.title: Vietnam opposes use of force against sovereign nations: Spokesperson
items.snippet: Vietnam calls upon all parties involved to act with responsibility, strictly adhere to international law, the United Nations Charter and relevant United Nations resolutions, respect the sovereignty and territorial integrity of nations, resume negotiations, and create favourable conditions for dialogue.

items.title: Vietnamese vessels urged to adopt highest security measures in Middle East waters
items.snippet: According to the latest update from the VIMAWA, as of late March 3, Vietnam has eight vessels with 160 crew members operating in the Middle East.

items.title: House bill seeks powers for President to suspend fuel excise tax
items.snippet: MANILA, Philippines — A bill that seeks to give the President powers to suspend the excise tax on petroleum products when warranted — like during the current conflict in the Middle East —was filed by Marikina Rep. Miro Quimbo on Tuesday. Quimbo, in a statement, said that he supports calls to suspend excise taxes on fue

items.title: Iran conflict could push up costs, govt’s fuel subsidy bill, say economists
items.snippet: Sunway University’s Yeah Kim Leng says disruptions in shipping due to the ongoing war will have cascading impacts.

items.title: Fuel shortage hits Shan State as imports from Thailand halt over Iran conflict
items.snippet: A protracted war in the Middle East could have a severe impact on energy supplies in Myanmar and the rest of the region

items.title: Empty shelves across Myanmar as Thai imports disappear
items.snippet: From snacks to medicine, goods once trucked across the Thai border have vanished following tightened import controls, border gate closures, and a crackdown on “illegal” trade

items.title: Vietnam tightens shipping safety, prepares contingency plans over Middle East risks
items.snippet: In a directive issued on March 3, VIMAWA Director Le Do Muoi instructed shipowners, crewing firms and shipping companies to conduct thorough risk assessments and adjust operations as needed, including preparing alternative shipping routes.

items.title: VinFast finalises three strategic vehicle brands, unveils two ultra-luxury models
items.snippet: Under the plan, VinFast’s automotive ecosystem is organised into three distinct brands - Lac Hong for ultra-luxury vehicles, VF for mass-market personal mobility, and Green for transport service operations, each targeting specific customer segments and supporting long-term development.

items.title: Two Indonesian oil tankers stranded in Persian Gulf
items.snippet: PT Pertamina International Shipping (PIS) currently has four vessels operating in the Middle East region. Of these, two vessels – Pertamina Pride and Gamsunoro – remain in the gulf. The other two have been relocated to relatively safer positions.

items.title: Deadly landfill collapse exposes risks faced by Philippines’ waste pickers
items.snippet: RODRIGUEZ, Philippines — Lenny* recalled freezing when he saw the first heap of garbage collapse underneath the feet of his fellow scavengers on the afternoon of Feb. 20, at a landfill in the town of Rodriguez, in the Philippines’ Rizal province. Moments later, a larger perimeter caved. In an instant, a crater of trash

items.title: Karenni IEC warns of ‘toxic’ levels of arsenic in Salween River and Molo Creek
items.snippet: Wastewater from mines has heavily contained both rivers, according to Karenni State’s resistance-run civilian authority
